name: Mom
supportFocus: Daily living and memory support — early-stage dementia with increasing confusion in the evenings
communicationPreferences: Short, calm sentences. Avoid correcting her on small things. Redirection works better than reasoning.
triggers: Loud noises or too many people
calmingStrategies: A warm cup of chamomile tea
Looking at old photo albums together
routines: Morning: breakfast at 8 AM, then a short walk
5 PM: calming music before dinner to ease sundowning
healthNotes: Early-stage Alzheimer's diagnosis (2024). On donepezil. Manages most ADLs independently with occasional reminders. GP reviews every 3 months.
whatHelps: Validation over correction. Keeping her environment familiar and predictable. Gentle touch and eye contact. Playing music she knows.
whatToAvoid: Arguing about what she remembers. Asking 'do you remember?' questions. Rushing her. Introducing too many changes at once.
